footer.based: Independent software studio based in Kyoto, Japan.

1.title: Reach out
1.desc: Tell us what you are trying to do, what is in your way, and any deadline. A rough note is enough; no particular format needed.

2.title: Scope
2.desc: A short call to check that your needs and what we can help with line up. If it looks right, we send scope, schedule, and an estimate in writing within a few days.

3.title: Build
3.desc: We work in short cycles, sharing a preview link and periodic notes so progress never goes dark.

4.title: Handover and aftercare
4.desc: On completion, take the code, docs, and deployment yourself, or let us keep running it. Support continues for a while after release; the relationship does not end with the invoice.

home.title: LASIKUU
home.metadescription: LASIKUU is an independent software studio in Kyoto, Japan. Forward-deployed and AI engineering, product building, systems integration, and technical advisory, in English, Japanese, and Finnish.
home.headline: Software built with the patience of moonlight.
home.lede: LASIKUU is an independent software studio in Kyoto. We embed directly with your team, ship the parts others stall on, and carry products from first design to final release.
home.s_services_lede: From embedding with your engineers and building with AI, to whole products, the systems that connect them, and the advice before the costly calls.
home.s_flow_lede: A calm, legible path from first message to delivery. No surprises, no theatre.

craft.title: Craft over volume
craft.body: We take on a focused set of projects at a time. The work gets the attention it needs, and the details are not an afterthought.

knowledge.title: Information that outlasts us
knowledge.body: We leave behind more than code: a clear record of what was decided and why. Architecture decision records and readable docs keep the knowledge with you long after the work.

direct.title: You talk to the builders
direct.body: No account managers between you and the people writing the code. Decisions are fast and questions get real answers.

trilingual.title: Three languages
trilingual.body: We work in English, Japanese, and Finnish, so projects that cross borders do not lose meaning in translation.

home.cta_body: Tell us what you are working on. A short message is enough to start; we will reply with honest next steps.

services.title: Services
services.metadescription: Forward-deployed engineering, AI engineering, product building, systems integration, and technical advisory from a Kyoto software studio.
services.headline: From engineering embedded in your team to the products and systems behind it.
services.lede: LASIKUU works end to end. Pick one piece or the whole arc, in English, Japanese, or Finnish.

embedded.title: Forward-deployed engineering
embedded.short: We embed with your team for a sprint or a season to ship the hard parts together.
embedded.long: When you need hands inside the team rather than a vendor at arm's length, we embed directly, remote day to day with on-site visits when they matter, for a sprint or a season. We learn your codebase, sit in your standups, and ship the difficult parts alongside your engineers.
embedded.tags: Sprint to season · Inside your codebase · Standups and reviews · Shipping the hard parts

ai.title: AI engineering
ai.short: AI built into products that ship, and into how your team works day to day.
ai.long: Two sides of the same craft. We build AI into products, language models, retrieval over your own data, assistants, and agent workflows, in the places where they earn their keep, with the plumbing around them done properly: evaluation, guardrails, cost control, and fallbacks. We also help existing teams adopt AI in their own work, introducing coding assistants and AI-assisted workflows for developers, with sensible practices instead of hype. When AI is the wrong tool, we will say so.
ai.tags: AI / LLM integration · Retrieval / RAG · Assistants and agents · Evaluation and guardrails · Cost control · AI workflows and best practices for dev teams

product.title: Product building
product.short: Products and features taken end to end, from design through release and improvement.
product.long: We take a product or feature from design through implementation, release, and the rounds of improvement after. A good fit for teams building out their engineering org, or at a stage where hiring a tech lead is hard. Web and backend, design and data, handled as one piece of work rather than a stack of separate invoices.
product.tags: API and backend · Database design · Realtime · Data pipelines · Automation scripts · Web apps · Mobile

integration.title: Systems integration
integration.short: Connecting the systems that should already be talking to each other.
integration.long: The work of making separate systems work as one: internal tools, AI workflows, and data flowing where it needs to. It often comes up in the middle of building a product, and it is just as often a job worth doing on its own.
integration.tags: Internal tools · AI workflows · Data sync · API integration · Auth and payments · Webhooks · Third-party services · Data migration

advisory.title: Technical advisory and consulting
advisory.short: An ongoing second opinion before the decisions that are expensive to undo.
advisory.long: A standing technical sounding board: architecture reviews, hiring, and AI strategy, for the moments when you want one more perspective before committing time and budget. We also put decisions on the record, with architecture decision records (ADRs) and clear documentation, so the reasoning behind a choice is still there when it matters. Useful as a one-off second opinion or as a continuing relationship, including steady upkeep once software is live.
advisory.tags: Architecture review · Decision records (ADR) · Documentation · AI strategy · Tech selection · Second opinions · Ongoing maintenance

services.pricing_lede: No hidden rate card games. Here is how an engagement is scoped and billed.

scope.title: Scope first
scope.body: We start with a short conversation, then a written estimate and plan. You see the shape and the budget before any work begins.

billing.title: Hourly, against a budget
billing.body: Work is billed hourly within an agreed project budget. One-time fixed engagements are possible when the scope is clear.

payment.title: Simple payment
payment.body: Credit card or bank transfer, processed through Wise. Japanese consumption tax applies for domestic transactions. Terms are set out in the contract.

languages.q: What languages do you work in?
languages.a: English, Japanese, and Finnish. Meetings, documents, and code comments can be in whichever fits your team best.

remote.a: Yes. Most work is remote, and we can also work on-site within Japan when a project calls for it, including forward-deployed engagements inside your team.

start.a: Send a short message by email, Telegram, or LINE describing what you are working on. We reply with a few questions, then a written estimate and plan.

ip.a: Unless we agree otherwise in the contract, the intellectual property of delivered work transfers to you. We are happy to put this in writing up front.

stack.a:
First of all, we adapt to whatever stack you are already on. We would rather fit your codebase than ask you to fit ours.

When we get to choose, we reach for TypeScript, Go, Rust, reactive Java, Kotlin, and Dart / Flutter, usually with PostgreSQL for storage. Services talk over REST, or Protocol Buffers and gRPC where it helps. On the front end, Svelte, React, Astro, or Vue. For infrastructure, Terraform and AWS CDK with containers, on bare metal, Hetzner, AWS, Google Cloud, or Cloudflare.

We keep up with the field and will pick the newest tools when they earn it, but only the stable ones, so you are not left on something that breaks or disappears.

size.a: No. LASIKUU is a focused, independent studio. You work directly with the people building your product, and we take on a deliberate number of projects at once so each one gets real attention.

about.title: About LASIKUU
about.headline: A Kyoto studio named after a Finnish moon.
about.content: LASIKUU is an independent software studio founded in March 2024 and based in Kyoto, Japan. Work is offered in English, Japanese, and Finnish, with the same hands staying on your project from first message to delivery.
about.name_explanation: The name is Finnish. “Lasi” is glass and “kuu” is moon: a glass moon. It stands for clarity and care, two things software too often lacks.
about.founder_p1: I studied computer science at the University of Helsinki and Kyoto University, then spent a few years building software at companies in Finland and Japan. I started LASIKUU to work directly with the people I build for, across the borders and languages my work tends to cross, and with the freedom to build things properly.
about.founder_p2: Today I work with clients internationally, in English, Japanese, and Finnish, designing and building software solutions and helping companies with technical strategy and decisions.
about.founder_p3: These are some of the values I keep close: fundamental system design and its architecture; keeping technical and comprehension debt from piling up; and cutting out the unnecessary to get working results to the customer fast.

contact.title: Contact
contact.headline: Let's talk about your project.
contact.content: Email or send a message on Telegram or LINE with a short note about what you are working on. Quotes and questions both welcome; we reply with honest next steps.

address.heading: Address
address.content: We will disclose without delay if requested.

tel.heading: Phone number
tel.content: We will disclose without delay if requested.

additionalfees.heading: Additional fees
additionalfees.content: Consumption tax (domestic) / credit card and bank transfer fees

cancelpolicy.heading: Cancellation (refund policy)
cancelpolicy.content: We do not accept cancellations after the contract. In the unlikely event of a cancellation during production, we will charge a cancellation fee according to the progress of the work. Also, even if no production work has been done, there will be no refund of the deposit after the contract has started.

returns.heading: Exchanges and returns policy
returns.content: Due to the nature of the service of providing data files and consulting, we cannot accept returns or exchanges.

delivery.heading: Delivery times and methods
delivery.content: We start planning and development at the time of contract. Delivery is done according to the contract terms.

payment.heading: Accepted payment methods
payment.content: Credit card or domestic/international bank transfer

paymentperiod.heading: Payment period
paymentperiod.content: Credit card payments are processed immediately, but bank transfers need to be made within 3 days of the order.

pricing.heading: Price
pricing.content: Varies by service. The price stated in the contract applies.

move.content: Moved to Japan to study and build a career in software.

simucube.title: Simucube, principal engineer and consultant

sugi.title: Sugi Holdings, lead engineer
sugi.content: Joined Sugi Holdings (スギホールディングス株式会社) as lead engineer on an international team, working full-stack on AWS.

tuner.title: Simucube Tuner 3.1 released
tuner.content: Simucube Tuner 3.1 shipped, with my work on the backend and cloud.

chevon.title: Chevon 5th Anniversary site released
chevon.content: The Chevon 5th Anniversary website went live, built and hosted by LASIKUU end to end, from the code to the infrastructure it runs on.
